example: The company must be cognizant of changing consumer preferences.
mnemonic: cogn(알다, 라틴어 cognoscere) + izant = 알고 있는 → 인식하는. cogn/gno(알다): recognize, cognition, diagnose

example: Excessive screen time can be a detriment to children's eye health.
mnemonic: de(떨어져) + tri(문지르다, 라틴어 terere) + ment = 문질러 닳게 하는 → 손해. trit(갈다): attrition, contrite

example: His irreverent humor often shocked the more conservative members.
mnemonic: ir(아닌) + reverent(공경하는) = 공경하지 않는 → 불경한

example: She was indignant about the unfair treatment she received at work.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + dign(가치 있는, 라틴어 dignus) + ant = 가치 없는 대우에 화난 → 분개한. dign(가치): dignity, disdain, indignation

example: The report contained a litany of complaints from dissatisfied customers.
mnemonic: 그리스어 litaneia(기도, 간청), lite(기도)에서 유래. 반복적인 기도 → 장황한 나열

example: The assistant's subservient behavior irritated the other team members.
mnemonic: sub(아래) + serv(섬기다) + ient = 아래에서 섬기는 → 비굴한. serv(섬기다): serve, servant, service

example: Her trenchant analysis exposed the flaws in the government's economic policy.
mnemonic: trench(자르다, 프랑스어 trancher) + ant = 자르는 듯한 → 날카로운. trench(자르다): entrench, trench

example: The film critiques the materialistic values of modern consumer society.
mnemonic: material(물질) + istic = 물질에 관한 → 물질주의적인. mater(물질, 어머니): matter, matrix

example: The rancorous feud between the two families lasted for generations.
mnemonic: rancor(원한, 라틴어 rancere=썩다) + ous = 썩은 감정이 가득한 → 원한에 찬

example: His callous disregard for others' feelings cost him many friendships.
mnemonic: callus(굳은살, 라틴어 callum) + ous = 굳은살이 박힌 → 감정이 무딘, 냉담한. 피부가 굳어지듯 마음도 굳어진 것

example: The cumbersome bureaucratic procedures delayed the project by months.
mnemonic: cumber(방해하다, 고대 영어) + some = 방해가 되는 → 다루기 힘든. encumber(방해하다)

example: The bucolic landscape of rolling hills and meadows inspired many painters.
mnemonic: 그리스어 boukolos(소치기), bous(소) + kolos(돌보다). 소를 돌보는 → 전원적인, 목가적인

example: The report enumerates the key factors contributing to climate change.
mnemonic: '이뉴머레이트' → 'e+numer(숫자)+ate' 숫자를 매겨 열거하다!

example: The conflagration destroyed an entire block of historic buildings.
mnemonic: con(완전히) + flagr(타다, 라틴어 flagrare) + ation = 완전히 타오르는 → 대화재. flagr(타다): flagrant(노골적인=불타듯 눈에 띄는)

example: She made only oblique references to her personal problems.
mnemonic: ob(향해) + lique(기울다) = 기울어진 → 비스듬한, 간접적인

example: The pusillanimous leader refused to make any controversial decisions.
mnemonic: pusill(작은, 라틴어 pusillus) + anim(정신) + ous = 정신이 작은 → 소심한. anim(정신): animate, unanimous

example: The chances of winning the lottery are infinitesimal.
mnemonic: in(아닌) + finit(끝, 한계) + esimal = 한계가 없을 만큼 작은 → 극소의. fin(끝): finite, infinite, define, final

example: His cupidity led him to embezzle millions from the charity.
mnemonic: cupid(욕망, 라틴어 cupiditas) + ity = 욕망 → 탐욕. Cupid(큐피드=욕망의 신). cup(갈망): covet

example: The musician's transcendent performance left the audience speechless.
mnemonic: trans(넘어서) + scend(오르다, 라틴어 scandere) + ent = 넘어 오르는 → 초월적인. scend(오르다): ascend, descend

example: The politician's grandiloquent promises failed to convince skeptical voters.
mnemonic: '그랜딜로퀀트' → 'grand(웅장)+loquent(말하는)' 웅장하게 과장 말하는!

example: She remained resolute in her decision despite heavy criticism.
mnemonic: re(완전히) + solut(풀다) + e = 완전히 풀어 결정한 → 단호한. solv(풀다): resolve, solution, dissolve

example: The gossamer wings of the dragonfly glistened in the sunlight.
mnemonic: goose(거위) + summer(여름). 가을 거위 잡는 시기에 떠다니는 거미줄 → 매우 가벼운, 섬세한

example: The pilot maintained remarkable sang-froid during the emergency landing.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 sang(피) + froid(차가운) = 차가운 피 → 침착함, 냉정

example: The student's turgid essay was filled with unnecessarily complex vocabulary.
mnemonic: turg(부풀다, 라틴어 turgere) + id = 부풀어 오른 → 과장된. turgor(팽압)

example: The general's ignoble surrender brought shame upon the entire army.
mnemonic: ig(아닌) + noble(고귀한) = 고귀하지 않은 → 비열한. nobil(고귀한): noble, ennoble, nobility

example: The providential arrival of reinforcements saved the besieged town.
mnemonic: pro(앞을) + vid(보다) + ential = 앞을 미리 보는 → 섭리의. vid(보다): provide, provident, evident

example: It is hard to envisage a world without the internet.
mnemonic: en(안에) + visage(얼굴, 모습) = 마음속에 모습을 그리다 → 상상하다. vis(보다): vision, visible, visage

example: The professor's soporific lecture put half the students to sleep.
mnemonic: sopor(깊은 잠, 라틴어) + ific = 잠을 만드는 → 졸리게 하는

example: The enormity of the crime shocked the entire nation.
mnemonic: e(밖으로) + norm(규범) + ity = 규범을 벗어남 → 극악함. norm(규범): normal, abnormal, enormous

ko: 옹호할 수 없는, 유지할 수 없는
example: His position became untenable after the evidence was made public.
mnemonic: un(아닌) + ten(잡다, 유지하다) + able = 유지할 수 없는 → 옹호 불가. ten(잡다): tenacious, retain, maintain

example: The veracious witness provided an accurate account of the events.
mnemonic: ver(진실, 라틴어 verus) + acious = 진실한. ver(진실): verify, verdict(진실을 말하다), very

example: The political party published a manifesto outlining its core beliefs.
mnemonic: manifest(명백한) + o = 명백하게 밝히는 것 → 선언문

example: The celebrity filed a lawsuit for defamation against the tabloid.
mnemonic: de(아래로) + fam(명성, 라틴어 fama) + ation = 명성을 떨어뜨림 → 명예훼손. fam(명성): famous, fame, infamous

example: The chef prepared a special menu for the delectation of his guests.
mnemonic: de(완전히) + lect(기쁘게 하다, 라틴어 delectare) + ation = 완전히 기쁘게 하는 것 → 즐거움. delectare: delectable, delight

example: After much debate, she finally acquiesced to the committee's decision.
mnemonic: ac(~쪽으로) + quiesce(조용해지다, 라틴어 quiescere) = 조용히 따르다 → 묵인하다. quie(조용한): quiet, quiescent

example: The retiring professor delivered a moving valediction at the graduation ceremony.
mnemonic: vale(안녕, 라틴어 valere=건강하다) + dict(말하다) + ion = 안녕이라고 말하는 것 → 고별사. val(건강): valid, valor. dict(말하다): dictate

example: She remonstrated with her son about his reckless spending habits.
mnemonic: re(다시) + monstr(보여주다, 라틴어 monstrare) + ate = 다시 보여주며 항의하다 → 항의하다. monstr(보여주다): demonstrate, monster

example: She gave an extemporaneous speech that impressed the entire audience.
mnemonic: '엑스템퍼레이니어스' → 'ex+tempor(시간)+aneous' 시간 밖의 즉흥적인!

example: The author added a parenthetical note explaining the historical context.
mnemonic: para(옆에) + en(안에) + thet(놓다) + ical = 옆에 넣은 → 삽입적인. parenthesis(괄호)의 형용사형

example: The empire subjugated neighboring tribes through military force.
mnemonic: sub(아래) + jug(멍에, 라틴어 jugum) + ate = 멍에 아래 두다 → 복종시키다. jug(멍에): conjugal(부부의=함께 멍에 진)

example: She cajoled her parents into letting her go to the concert.
mnemonic: 프랑스어 cajoler에서 유래, 라틴어 cavea(새장)과 관련. 새를 새장에 꾀어 넣듯 → 감언이설로 구슬리다

example: She spoke with unexpected asperity about the proposal.
mnemonic: asper(거친, 라틴어) + ity = 거친 상태 → 가혹함. exasperate(격분시키다)와 같은 어근

example: The manipulation of data in the study invalidated the research findings.
mnemonic: mani(손, 라틴어 manus) + pul(채우다) + ation = 손으로 다루는 것 → 조작. man(손): manual, manuscript, manufacture

example: The prisoner supplicated the judge for mercy.
mnemonic: sub(아래에) + plic(접다, 라틴어 plicare) + ate = 아래에서 무릎 꿇다 → 간청하다. plic(접다): complicate, implicit, supple

example: The politician tried to obfuscate the issue with irrelevant details.
mnemonic: ob(위에) + fusc(어두운) + ate = 어둡게 만들다 → 혼란시키다

example: The vitriolic comments on social media revealed deep divisions in society.
mnemonic: vitriol(황산, 라틴어 vitriolum=유리) + ic = 황산처럼 부식시키는 → 신랄한

example: The workers earned a mere pittance for twelve hours of hard labor.
mnemonic: 라틴어 pietas(경건함), 수도원에서 자선으로 주는 적은 음식에서 유래 → 적은 돈

example: A deep lassitude overcame her after weeks of working without rest.
mnemonic: lass(지친, 라틴어 lassus) + itude = 지친 상태 → 피로. alas(아, 슬프다)와 같은 어감

example: The judge warned the defendant that levity was inappropriate in the courtroom.
mnemonic: lev(가벼운, 라틴어 levis) + ity = 가벼움 → 경솔함. lev(가벼운): lever, elevate, alleviate, levitate

example: A cursory glance at the report revealed several obvious errors.
mnemonic: curs(달리다, 라틴어 currere) + ory = 달리듯 빨리 지나가는 → 대충의. curr/curs(달리다): cursor, current, occur
